script_excerpt.label: NR_01
script_excerpt.original_english: The factory closed on a Friday afternoon in March. By Monday morning, 2,400 people had lost their jobs.
script_excerpt.translation: La fábrica cerró un viernes por la tarde en marzo. Para el lunes por la mañana, 2,400 personas habían perdido sus empleos.

script_excerpt.label: NR_02
script_excerpt.original_english: Nobody saw it coming. The company had posted record profits the year before.
script_excerpt.translation: Nadie lo vio venir. La empresa había registrado ganancias récord el año anterior.

script_excerpt.label: NR_03
script_excerpt.original_english: What followed was not just unemployment. It was the collapse of a community that had been built, generation by generation, around a single employer.
script_excerpt.translation: Lo que siguió no fue solo desempleo. Fue el colapso de una comunidad que se había construido, generación tras generación, alrededor de un solo empleador.

script_excerpt.label: NR_01
script_excerpt.original_english: She had been waiting for this moment her entire life.
script_excerpt.translation: Elle avait attendu ce moment toute sa vie.

input.broadcast_standard: EBU R 128 compliant, no faster than 16 syllables/second

script_excerpt.label: NR_01
script_excerpt.original_english: The European Union's carbon trading system was supposed to make pollution expensive. Instead, it created a market where the right to pollute could be bought and sold.
script_excerpt.translation: Das Emissionshandelssystem der Europäischen Union sollte die Verschmutzung teuer machen. Stattdessen schuf es einen Markt, auf dem das Recht zu verschmutzen gekauft und verkauft werden konnte.

script_excerpt.label: NR_02
script_excerpt.original_english: By 2023, the price of carbon had peaked at one hundred euros per tonne.
script_excerpt.translation: Bis 2023 hatte der Preis für Kohlenstoff einen Höchststand von hundert Euro pro Tonne erreicht.

script_excerpt.label: NR_03
script_excerpt.original_english: Critics called it a licence to pollute. Supporters called it the most ambitious climate instrument in history. The truth, as with most European compromises, was somewhere in between — and satisfying to nobody.
script_excerpt.translation: Kritiker nannten es eine Lizenz zum Verschmutzen. Befürworter nannten es das ambitionierteste Klimainstrument der Geschichte. Die Wahrheit lag, wie bei den meisten europäischen Kompromissen, irgendwo dazwischen — und befriedigte niemanden.
